projects.subtitle: Audioreactive Light Installation
projects.title: Koda Kosmos live on tape
projects.credits: Florian Seefing – Lighting Design/Operating, Daniel Olbricht – Musician
projects.description: A live multi-camera production at Landschaftspark Duisburg-Nord, realized in cooperation with Hochschule Düsseldorf. I designed the stage setup and developed the visual system: real-time visuals were generated and mapped onto 60+ wireless LED tubes, driven directly by the duo's live synthesizer performance. Show control, audio reactivity, and pixel mapping were handled by TAPE, a custom framework I built.

projects.role: Light Designer, Creative Direction, TouchDesigner Developer
projects.subtitle: Audiovisual Lightshow
projects.title: ELEO
projects.description: With a unique fusion of electronic club music and gentle vocals, ELEO creates a sonic world where every emotion finds its place. With self-developed pixel tubes, beam bars, and mirrors, the music becomes a live visual experience. Innovative lighting work illuminates expansive synthesizer sounds in detail-oriented productions, turning the performance into a multisensory experience.

projects.subtitle: Audiovisual Performance
projects.title: manipulation
projects.description:
"manipulation" takes a DNA sequence as its starting point. Through hand movements, the performer intervenes directly in the audiovisual system — shaping sound and image in real time. The work traces the tension between biological structure and digital logic, asking what it means to influence something you yourself have set in motion.
Made with Ableton, TouchDesigner and Leapmotion Sensor

projects.subtitle: Fulldome Performance
projects.title: &#X1UFFFE
projects.credits: Emil Theodor Felhofer – Direction, dance, sound. Sasha Krutikova – Dance, sound, choreography
projects.description:
A symbiosis of dance, sound, sensory experience, and visuals.
Sound and visuals respond in real time to the dancers, driven by body sensors.

projects.title: TAPE – TouchDesigner Ableton Performance Engine
projects.subtitle: Bachelor Thesis Software
projects.description: TAPE is a show control framework that bridges Ableton Live and TouchDesigner for audiovisual live performance. Built on top of TDAbleton, it synchronizes Ableton's timeline structure with a hierarchical visual network, providing normalized data buses for audio level, MIDI, beat, and parameter automation. Visual modules bind to these buses in real time, allowing generative content, live camera input, video playback, and pixel mapping pipelines to respond directly to the music. TAPE does not generate visual content itself, but provides the structural and reactive infrastructure on which a complete audiovisual show can be built.

projects.subtitle: Interactive Software
projects.title: Deutsche Telekom – MWC 2026
projects.description: For Deutsche Telekom's presence at Mobile World Congress 2026 in Barcelona, I developed the TouchDesigner software for two interactive exhibits on behalf of the experience design studio GROSSE8: The Magenta AI Call Assistant and the Quantum Teleportation installation. Both exhibits translated complex technological innovations into tangible, hands-on experiences for a global trade show audience. The setups were designed for reliable performance in a demanding live event environment over the course of the congress.

projects.title: DIY Pixel Tubes
projects.subtitle: Custom Build
projects.description: A hardware project in which I designed and built eight 2-metre, high-brightness, high-resolution pixel tubes including their controllers

projects.subtitle: Interactive Art Installation
projects.title: (Prototype) Plant Designer
projects.credits: Project by: Konrad Simon, Mathias Wensauer, Marika Ajima, Tobia Diekmann
projects.description:
In this interactive installation, visitors can explore and create personalized organic geo-metries based on three different L-system algorithms. L-systems (Lindenmayer systems)
are a mathematical model used to describe the growth of plants and other natural forms through recursive rules and geometric patterns.

Using rotary controls and sensors, participants manipulate parameters to generate unique plant-inspired forms, which can be printed as tangible artworks. The installation encourages playful exploration of algorithmic design and the intersection of nature and technology.

Made with TouchDesigner and Arduino

projects.title: Joystick Controller
projects.subtitle: Custom Build
projects.description: A custom-built 4-axis MIDI joystick controller for expressive live performance. Designed for parameters like pitch bend and expression, it uses an ESP32 microcontroller and a 3D-printed enclosure.
